description: June 23 is the last day for students to file Graduation Petitions.

title: Library Learning for All Abilities
description: Autistic youth and young people with disabilities age 15-21 are invited to develop job skills and serve the community in a fun and supportive environment. Kind and friendly teens without disabilities who want to develop skills while supporting their peers are also being recruited. Please register for all sessions to participate fully. All participants receive service hours as part of Teen Summer Service. Fill out this contact form to be added to our list of interested teen volunteers and be one of the first to know about volunteer opportunities.Email the teen team to learn more or to express an interest in volunteering.

title: Breezy Books and Boba
description: Explore some of the books on our Breezy Reads list through read-alouds and activities and enjoy a variety of flavors of bubble tea and boba. Kids younger than eight must have an adult in the library during the event.  Allergy notice: Drinks may contain common allergens or ingredients such as dairy, soy, or artificial flavorings. Ingredient information will be available.

title: Cool Story
description: Chat about some of this year's Cool Reads and then dig into your artistic side with creative writing exercises. Kids in grade 3-6 attend on their own.

title: A Brief History of Skokie
description: Illinois became a state in 1818, but even by the beginning of the 1830s, Chicago, as the largest non-Indian settlement, only had about 150 inhabitants. Learn about the beginnings of Niles Township, the first settlers in the area and their relationship to the Native Americans, and how this place would eventually become Skokie -- with a new Village seal and a new Village flag!

title: Baby Playtime
description: This drop-in playgroup is for babies age 0 to 23 months. Babies can play with developmentally appropriate toys while their grown-ups socialize with other parents and caregivers.Siblings are welcome. Free tickets will be given to those who attend.

title: Morning Movie
description: We Bought a ZooBenjamin Mee, recently widowed with two children—14-year-old Dylan and 7-year-old Rosie—thinks that starting over will do them all good, especially after Dylan gets expelled from school. While looking to relocate, they find a dilapidated old house that happens to come with a zoo that needs saving. There, they meet zookeeper Kelly Foster and her small, dedicated staff, who want to see it reopened. Despite the huge financial commitment and having no experience, Benjamin takes on the task with the help of his family.Loosely inspired by a true story, this is a healing adventure about second chances. Starring Matt Damon, Scarlett Johansson, Thomas Haden Church, Patrick Fugit, and Elle Fanning. Directed by Cameron Crowe.2011 : 124 minutes : Rated PG for language and some thematic elements : Comedy-dramaFree tickets will be given to all who attend. Watch the trailer for We Bought a Zoo.Find We Bought a Zoo in the library catalog.

title: ELL Café
description: Practice speaking English with other members of the community. Volunteer tutors will help with conversations and answer questions. Free tickets will be given to those who attend.Keyword: Adult Learners

title: Who Was...? American History Bingo
description: Let's celebrate America's 250th birthday with two of our favorite things: the Who Was...? series of children's biographies and bingo. Designed for kids in grade K-5 with a grown-up.At least one adult per family must attend, and each person must register. This event is also being held Friday, July 3, 4:30-5:30 pm. Please register for only session.Keyword: USA 250

title: Summer Storytime
description: Enjoy a fun all-ages storytime. For children with accompanying adults. Space is limited. Tickets will be distributed to those present starting at 9:30 am.

title: Crochet Basics
description: Learn basic crochet terminology and how to crochet in this introductory class. Materials will be provided, and supplies may be limited. You are welcome to bring your own hooks or yarn.

title: Using Transportation Applications
description: There are many options for getting from place to place, and many can be accessed on your phone. This presentation will review various apps and resources such as Uber, Lyft, Google Maps, and the CTA transit app.

title: Family Storytime
description: Children and their grown-ups participate together in this storytime with books, music, and more.Space is limited. Free tickets will be given to those present starting at 6 pm. Children younger than eight must be accompanied by an adult.

title: Melody and Motion
description: This musical storytime is perfect for kids who are always on the go. Every session is full of songs, rhymes, and movement.Free tickets will be given to those present starting at 9:30 am. For kids age 0-6 with an adult. Space is limited.

title: Share Your Immigrant Heritage
description: Share your family's immigration story to help celebrate Immigrant Heritage Month. By seeing the history of our community through the forces that brought us into proximity--like opportunity, family ties, or refuge--we can appreciate where we come from, how migration changes us and our communities, and where we might be going. More opportunities to share: Wednesday, June 10, 11 am-1 pm and 3-5 pmMonday, June 15, 11 am-1 pm and 4-6 pmFriday, June 26, 2-4 pm

title: Bookworms
description: Book lovers who want to help their community and make the library even more awesome can help create displays, read books before they hit the shelves, and explore where librarians make the magic happen.All participants receive service hours as part of Teen Summer Service. Fill out this contact form to be added to our list of interested teen volunteers and be one of the first to know about volunteer opportunities.Email the teen team to learn more or to express an interest in volunteering. For grade 6-12.

title: International Snack Down
description: Experience different countries through snacks. Each month we'll be getting a mystery box of snacks from another country. Do a taste test to see what you like best, learn some fun facts about the country, and have fun! Kids in grade 3-6 attend on their own. Registration limited to 20 kids.Because this is a subscription service, we cannot guarantee allergen-free options.

title: Family Movie
description: MigrationThe Mallard family decides to leave their safe pond in New England and fly to Jamaica for a vacation in this delightful, animated adventure that blends laugh-out-loud moments with heartwarming lessons about family. Plus, you'll love the voice cast and fun visuals.Featuring the voice talents of Kumail Nanjiani, Elizabeth Banks, Caspar Jennings, Tresi Gazal, Awkwafina, Carol Kane, Keegan-Michael Key, David Mitchell, and Danny DeVito. Directed by Benjamin Renner.2023 : 83 minutes : Rated PG for action/peril and mild rude humor : AnimationFree tickets will be given to all who attend.Watch the trailer for Migration.Find Migration in the library catalog.

title: Saturday Family Storytime
description: Enjoy a fun all-ages storytime. For children with accompanying adults. Free tickets will be given to those present starting at 10:30 am. Space is limited.

title: Deadline to register for summer 2026 classes to be considered for financial aid
description: June 28 is the deadline to register for summer 2026 classes to be considered for financial aid. Financial aid will not be available for students who register after this date.

title: Get to Know the Grand Old Flag
description: With the upcoming 4th of July celebrating the 250th anniversary of the United States, learn about the history of the U.S. flag with expert Marty Callahan. He'll share other historic flags that will inspire you as we make our own creations and parade them around the library. Parade will begin at 12:15 pm. Keyword: USA 250

title: Rohingya Music in Exile--Courage, Faith, and Love
description: The Crossing Borders Music String Quartet shares brand-new arrangements of Rohingya music and its stories of courage, faith, and love in exile. Developed after a month of field work in the Rohingya refugee camps of Cox's Bazar, Bangladesh-- the world's largest--this performance shares the melodies, hope, and humanity of Rohingya musicians who are the keepers of their people’s stories and cultural legacy. Transcend physical distance with music that helps everyone "see the Rohingya with our hearts" and celebrates our shared humanity. Free tickets will be distributed to those present at 2:30 pm.

title: Monarch Community Science Project
description: Monarch butterflies play an important role as pollinators, and their populations are in trouble. Teen volunteers will learn about them and teach others, too.Tend our native garden patch, observe monarch eggs and caterpillars, and talk with other library patrons about your observations and how we can all support monarchs. Data will be submitted to the Monarch Larva Monitoring Project. All participants receive service hours as part of Teen Summer Service. All participants receive service hours as part of Teen Summer Service. Fill out this contact form to be added to our list of interested teen volunteers and be one of the first to know about volunteer opportunities.Email the teen team to learn more or to express an interest in volunteering. For grade 6-12.

title: Road Trip Across the USA
description: Celebrate USA's 250th birthday with a fun, hands-on journey across the country. We'll explore the 50 states through stories, games, and movement activities. Travel from region to region and collect stamps along the way—no suitcase required! Free tickets will be given to all who attend. For grade K-2. Kids younger than eight must have an adult in the library during the event.   Keyword: USA 250

title: Teen STEAM Team
description: Design STEAM challenges that will allow kids to experiment and compete. In this session, collaborate with other teen volunteers to mess around with the materials and learn some hands-on teaching methods.Then, on Wednesday, June 17, help lead kids in grade 3-5 in STEAM activities. Finally, apply what you learned with another project, meeting on June 30 and July 1. Volunteers must commit to all four dates.  All participants receive service hours as part of Teen Summer Service. Fill out this contact form to be added to our list of interested teen volunteers and be one of the first to know about volunteer opportunities.Email the teen team to learn more or to express an interest in volunteering. For grade 9-12.

title: The Constitution Today
description: Political analyst Paul Lisnek moderates a nonpartisan panel discussion with Chicagoland university professors. They'll discuss current issues around the First Amendment, power structure of the federal branches, and recent cases heard by the Supreme Court.About the Moderator: Paul Lisnek served as the political and legal analyst for WGN-TV for 18 years. Paul has been a lawyer for 40 years, is a former assistant dean and professor at Loyola Law School, and taught at DePaul University, University of Illinois in Urbana, and Pepperdine University. He teaches constitutional law and ethics for the lawyers bar exam. He was a disciplinary commissioner for lawyers for 25 years, and he served as a legal and jury expert for NBC News and CNN. PanelistsSteven D. Schwinn teaches constitutional law, First Amendment, comparative constitutional law and human rights, and lawyering skills for civil rights at UIC Law.Robert E. Shapiro is a trial lawyer and litigator at Barack Ferrazzano Kirschbaum & Nagelberg LLP with more than 40 years of experience. He handles commercial disputes in trial and appellate courts, ADR forums, and administrative and governmental proceedings nationwide and abroad. He is an adjunct professor of philosophy at Saint Xavier UniversityThis event is part of the Civic Empowerment Series 2026, in partnership with Schaumburg Township District Library, Vernon Area Public Library, Blue Island Public Library, Highland Park Public Library, Aurora Public Library District, La Grange Public Library, Fox River Valley Public Library, Algonquin Area Public Library District, and Oak Park Public Library. Register and receive a Zoom link for this event.For questions about this event, call Schaumburg Township District Library at 847-923-3347.
